context: <<POS:36548719>> createPerk("Musky Pheromones",0,0,0,0,"Grants a +5 musk bonus to tease attacks.")

original: Just as your thoughts were turning to your rotund and overbearing belly, you feel something in your loins, followed by a rush in your lower stomach. It’s time; the kip’s on its way right now.
You reach for the nearest medkit before hurrying to your quarters to set yourself on your bed. You get as comfortable as you can in your hurry,
You flag down the nearest civilian and tell them the situation, and that you’re in some pretty dire need of medical assistance. You find a safe place to sit down,
You’re in a less-than-ideal environment for this to be happening, but you don’t have a lot of time to be complaining. You find a safe place to sit yourself down,
then struggle to remove your [pc.lowerGarments], then
you splay your legs, then you push.

You lack a lot of conscious input in the entire procedure; your body knows what it needs to do and you don’t try to hold yourself back just because you’re not in a hospital or a nursery. Although the situation is no less urgent than any other pregnancy, the whole ordeal is otherwise quite smooth and it’s not nearly as painful as you were expecting – mostly because you’re not pushing out a very small Frostwyrm, but because you’re pushing out an egg the size of [pc.aOriginalRace] baby.

When the scenario is over, you aren’t left with a kicking, screaming, breathing bundle of joy, but a very large, sturdy egg. It’s pure white all over, with no distinguishing markings around it. In a way, you’re kind of relieved to know that your crossbreeding with Nykke resulted in a perfectly normal Frostwyrm egg, and not something smaller or differently-shaped, and the baby inside it is likely just as healthy.

The last concern is answered when you hear the telltale melodic sounds, still in your head: despite being physically separated from you, the kip inside is still psionically bonded with you enough to communicate in its wordless way.

You call for a transit ship to take the egg"+("uveto vii"===n?"":" to Uveto, and program in the coordinates for the shoreline rather than the landing dock")+". Your thoughts turn to Nykke; you wonder if you should take the egg there yourself, so that you could call for her and have her take the egg from you personally.

You have lain?! you hear Nykke say suddenly, startling you. You never liked it when [frostwyrm.name] did that to you, and you were hoping Nykke wouldn’t make a habit of it too. And our egg is on its way?! But how? Are you not on our world? I know that your kind can travel the stars–

Nykke’s communication stops suddenly, then starts again once she’s collected herself. I will not pretend to understand the specifics, but you tell when and where to find our egg, and I will fly there with a speed that [frostwyrm.name] wishes she could manage.

When the transit ship arrives, you load the heavy egg into its crib. It was built for a living creature, but it houses your egg sturdily. When the ship leaves, you reach out to Nykke, and inform her that the egg should arrive before the end of the day – or, uh, before Light’s Fall.
With all of that squared away, you shakily bring yourself to your [pc.feet]. You’re a good amount of weight lighter, but that still knocked all the energy out of you. You should... probably lie down.
